title: Thermophotovoltaic efficiency of 40%

claim_supported: 1.4/1.2 eV tandem TPV cell reached 41.1+/-1% efficiency at 2.39 W/cm^2 power density and 2400C emitter temperature; 1.2/1.0 eV device reached 39.3+/-1% at 2127C -- record TPV efficiencies exceeding typical steam-turbine efficiency.

claim_supported: Taiwan-based Chinese-language coverage (dated 2023-11-02) of the ribbon-cutting for the US DOE/Southwest Research Institute (SwRI)/GTI Energy/GE Vernova sCO2 turbine demonstration in San Antonio: a table-sized turbine producing 10 million watts (enough for ~10,000 households), 10x smaller and ~10% more thermally efficient than an equivalent steam turbine, 2-minute startup vs. 30 minutes for steam, total project cost cited as $155 million.
locator: Article body, chinatimes.com, fetched directly this session.
notes: CLAIM CORRECTED: the raw scout record mischaracterized this as coverage of "a supercritical CO2 turbine developed in China"; direct fetch this session confirms the article is actually Taiwan Chinese-language reporting on the SAME US STEP Demo / SwRI facility already covered in L04-039 (US DOE, SwRI, GTI Energy, GE Vernova consortium, San Antonio location), not a Chinese-developed turbine. Geography corrected from ["TW","CN"] to ["TW","US"] accordingly (CN removed -- no Chinese entity or facility is involved). Retained for Taiwan-geography, Chinese-language media-coverage diversity of a US facility; the $155M/10MW figures differ somewhat from L04-039's $169M/~4MW figures (likely reflect different project milestones or media rounding) and should not be treated as an independent confirmation of L04-039's numbers.

institutions.name: China Times (Want Want China Times Media Group)

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: Taiwan-based Chinese-language media outlet.

title: sCO2 Turbo Expander Generator (TEG) / sCO2 Engine product brochure
authors_or_org: Hanwha Power Systems
claim_supported: Hanwha Power Systems (South Korea) markets a commercial sCO2 Turbo Expander Generator (TEG) / sCO2 Engine product line for waste-heat-to-power applications; the brochure PDF (9MB) was confirmed live and fetchable this session (the original hanwhapowersystems.com host 301-redirects to hanwhapower.com), but its binary/embedded-font PDF structure could not be parsed into readable text by the fetch tool, so specific price and performance-spec figures remain unconfirmed.
locator: PDF confirmed live via WebFetch redirect this session (m.hanwhapower.com/static/data/brochure/sCO2_8P_EN_final.pdf, 301 redirect from the original m.hanwhapowersystems.com URL); binary content not machine-readable by the fetch tool.
notes: URL updated to the redirected working host (hanwhapower.com, not hanwhapowersystems.com) since the original host now forwards there. Kept as a vendor_datasheet lead for the market/industry-adjacent quota; a human or a PDF-text-extraction-capable tool should open the file directly to extract price/spec figures before treating any number from it as confirmed.
